_metadata.title: Continuing Education Requirements by Profession and State
_metadata.description: CE renewal requirements for Social Workers, Psychologists, Marriage & Family Therapists, and Counselors across all 50 US states + DC
_metadata.disclaimer: Requirements change frequently. Always verify with the official state licensing board before relying on this data for compliance purposes.

specific_requirements: Courses must be NASW, ASWB, CSWE, or board-approved
Alabama.board: Alabama State Board of Social Work Examiners
Alabama.notes: Verify current requirements with state board

specific_requirements: Must be relevant to social work practice

Arizona.notes: Verify current requirements with state board

specific_requirements: 6 hours suicide risk assessment (one-time, first renewal after 1/1/2021)
Coursework on aging and long-term care
ASWB, APA, or NBCC approved providers
California.notes: First renewal: 18 hours minimum. Supervision training required if supervising.

specific_requirements: Approved by NASW, ASWB, CSWE, or other recognized bodies

specific_requirements: 2 hours on mental health conditions of veterans (first renewal, then every 6 years)

specific_requirements: LCSWs: 6 hours ethics; LSWs/LMSWs: 3 hours ethics

specific_requirements: 6 hours in professional ethics and law
2 hours in professional roles and boundaries

specific_requirements: Up to 6 hours can carry over to next year

specific_requirements: 4 hours in child abuse or domestic violence training
Category I must be APA, state-sponsored, or accredited
APA or other national mental health organization approved
Per CPD (Continuing Professional Development) model as of 1/1/2024
APA, CMA, ACCMA, or CA Psychological Association approved

specific_requirements: APA, university, or professional organization approved
Connecticut.board: Connecticut Board of Examiners of Psychologists

specific_requirements: 2 hours domestic violence (every third renewal / 6 years)
6 hours ethics at a personally attended workshop

specific_requirements: APA or state psychological association approved
Up to 20 hours carry over to next year
Home study must have APA or similar sponsorship
6 hours ethics, Iowa laws/rules, or risk management (2nd renewal and after)
APA approval accepted but not required

Kansas.online_limit: Max 40 hours directed self-study; max 12 hours non-directed self-study

specific_requirements: APA or other listed professional organization approved

Louisiana.renewal_cycle: Biennial (odd licenses odd years, even licenses even years)

specific_requirements: No continuing education required by state law
APA courses automatically approved; accreditation not strictly required

specific_requirements: Prescribing psychologists: 60 hours total, 40 in psychopharmacology

specific_requirements: 3 hours professional ethics including NY laws and rules
New York.board: New York State Office of the Professions: Psychology

specific_requirements: Min 9 hours Category A (formally organized, including online)
North Carolina.online_limit: No specific limit for online (counts as Category A)

specific_requirements: APA, CPA, or other listed boards/associations

specific_requirements: 4 hours professional conduct, ethics, or culture/ethnic identity
Refer to Oklahoma Board for current details

specific_requirements: 4 hours ethics or Oregon state laws and rules
Refer to Rhode Island Board for current details

specific_requirements: No more than 30 hours on single topic
APA or Vermont Psychological Association pre-approved
Vermont.online_limit: Max 24 hours individual activities including online

specific_requirements: 6 hours suicide prevention (from Model List, required once every 6 years)
